message:
test(e2e): add diagnostic spec asserting Scroll-to-Explore never overlaps hero stat chips on mobile

Adds `tests/e2e/_diag-scroll-indicator.spec.ts` — one mobile-viewport
(375×800) Playwright test per article (wills-estate, slack-migration,
overprompting, raptorq, barra-factor-model) that:

1. Loads the article and waits for hydration.
2. Screenshots the `[data-section="hero"]` block for visual evidence at
   `/tmp/wills-audit/hero-mobile-<slug>.png`.
3. Queries the DOM for the `Scroll to Explore` span's parent bounding
   rect and the last stat-chip bounding rect.
4. Asserts `scrollIndicator.top >= lastStatChip.bottom - 1`, i.e. the
   indicator sits at or below the bottom edge of the last chip with at
   most one pixel of slack.

This catches the class of regression fixed in the preceding commit — any
change that reintroduces absolute positioning on the mobile breakpoint
would cause the two blocks to vertically intersect and the test to fail
loudly instead of shipping a hero with overlapping, illegible text.

Follows the established `_diag-` convention in `tests/e2e/`: creates
its `/tmp/wills-audit` output dir with `mkdirSync({recursive: true})`,
pins `baseURL` via `test.use`, and uses relative URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
